title: Conic Discriminant FAIL: falsifier with mislabeled conic type and wrong convergent I
_primary_source_note: Intentional falsifier for validator testing (not a new claim), same discriminant theory as the PASS fixtures (Hardy, 2008) continued fractions and (Wildberger, 2005) chromogeometry.
_fixture_purpose: Falsifier: declares hyperbola witness (3,2) with conic_type='ellipse' (should be hyperbola since I=7>0, trips CD_3 and the hyperbola-witness I>0 check), and a convergent (5,2) declared I=999 (should be -1, trips the convergent-sequence check). NOTE: result is deliberately 'PASS' internally because this validator's validate() short-circuits to skip all checking when result=='FAIL' -- matches the pattern used in certs [214]/[144]/[141].

general_theorem.type_rule: I>0 ↔ hyperbola; I=0 ↔ parabola (impossible for integers); I<0 ↔ ellipse
general_theorem.threshold: d/e = 1+√2 (silver ratio) separates hyperbola from ellipse

hyperbola_witnesses.check: FALSIFIER: I=12-5=7>0 is hyperbola, declared ellipse

convergent_sequence.pattern: FALSIFIER: convergent (5,2) declares I=999, should be -1 (I=20-21=-1)

validation_checks.description: FALSIFIER: (3,2) declared ellipse but I=7>0 is hyperbola

fail_ledger: CD_3: hyperbola witness (3,2) has I=7 declared as ellipse (also caught by hyperbola-witness I>0 sanity check since ellipse-labeled but genuinely I>0)
